readerTask: Explain why Ponytail callbacks stalled and what the repaired operating shape is, with complete portable Markdown plus an interactive walkthrough.
requiredFacts: Callbacks were created through the stock queue path; a new stock callback regression was not established.
Busy controller topology increased queue pressure and removed a safe continuation path.
The repaired kernel dispatches one callback-owned worker and returns the exact coordinator session idle.
A watchdog is exceptional recovery and must not become a competing writer.
inferences: Idle posture reduces exposure to dropped or delayed queue-drain triggers.
limitations: A successful callback task does not alone prove correct reconciliation.
The kernel canary does not prove restart recovery.
decisionBoundary: The explanation may recommend the topology but cannot authorize production promotion.
prohibitedOverclaims: Historical callback completion proves every workflow continued correctly.

questions.q: Where did the observed failure occur?
questions.a: After callback admission, when queued work failed to drain or no safe controller continuation remained.

questions.q: What is the normal repaired loop?
questions.a: One exact coordinator dispatches one callback-owned worker, returns idle, reconciles the callback, takes one next action, then returns idle.

questions.q: What remains unproven by a no-watchdog kernel pass?
questions.a: Recovery across restart wipes, silent stopped children, and dropped drain triggers.
Only its bounded recovery receipt or nudge; not objective truth or board state.
